PLANNER / BONUS / TECH STACK PLANNER
HOW DO I KNOW WHAT TOOLS I NEED?
✓ 	The starting place for figuring out what tools you need is to make a list of
the features you need for what you want to accomplish. Remember that
you may not find one tool that ticks all the boxes; so you need to decide
what features are most important to you and choose your tools from there.
We recommend using a conservative technology strategy: Invest in
technology as you need it for the next stage or two of your business then
add and upgrade as you go along (a powerful set of tools that you are
months or years away from being able to utilize is usually not a good
investment).
What are the features you need?
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
✓ 	Once you have a list of the features you need for what you want to
accomplish, decide on whether or not you’re going to go with an all-in-
one solution or an a la carte solution. We recommend an a la carte
approach; in most cases it’s better to add to your stack (or upgrade to new
tools over time) than to buy a full-fledged solution (and pay thousands of
dollars) before you need it.
All-in-one or A la Carte?

PLANNER / BONUS / TECH STACK PLANNER
HOW DO I SELECT THE RIGHT TOOLS?
There are 8 criteria we suggest you look at as you narrow down your
technology options. Use this as you evaluate each tool; the more each tool
meets these criteria, the more likely it will be a tool that you’ll be happy with.
1. 	FUNCTIONALITY
✓ 	Does the tool do what you need it to do?
✓ 	Will you be able to use all or most of the features (if there are many
features you don’t need or aren’t like to use soon, it may not be the right
tool)?
Notes:
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
2. 	INTEGRATIONS
✓ 	Will the tool work with other tools in your stack? Look at the “integrations”
section of the providers website; ideally you want an API integration.
✓ 	Can the service provider answer specific questions about how their tool
integrates with your existing tools?
Notes:
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	of	4 	10
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 10 --

PLANNER / BONUS / TECH STACK PLANNER
3. 	FLEXIBILITY
✓ 	Does the company have various options for how to configure the tool? You
ideally want a tool that gives you as much flexibility as possible.
Notes:
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
4. INVESTMENT
✓ 	Does the company offer packages that let you scale (e.g. “Starter”, “Pro”,
“Enterprise”, etc.)?
✓ 	Have you checked to see if there are on-boarding/implementation fees?
✓ 	Is there a discount if you buy an annual membership?
Notes:
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
5. 	REPUTABILITY
✓ 	Does the company have good reviews/testimonials?
✓ 	Is the company regularly upgrading and improving their technology to
meet changing trends and demands?
© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	of	5 	10

6. TRIAL PERIOD/GUARANTEE
✓ 	Does the company have a money-back guarantee or free trial period (it’s
really helpful to get behind the wheel before you fully commit)?
Notes:
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
7. 	SUPPORT
✓ 	Does the company offer live support?
✓ 	Are you happy with the support hours and how soon you can expect a
response if you run into problems?
✓ 	Are you happy with the support channels available (some companies only
offer email and chat, but some also offer phone support)?
Notes:
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________

8. TRAINING
✓ 	Can the support staff show you exactly how the tool works?
✓ 	Does the company have a knowledge base?
✓ 	Do they offer on-boarding training?
✓ 	Do they have webinars, videos, tutorials or other educational training
tools?

PLANNER / BONUS / TECH STACK PLANNER
HOW DO I SET UP MY TOOLS?
There are 5 steps to implementing your technology tools:
✓ 	Select and purchase your tools
✓ 	Create your account
✓ 	Add the end of trial date/refund date to your calendar (if applicable)
✓ 	Download and install the tool (if applicable)
✓ 	Set aside time to complete any training provided
✓ 	Calendar to periodically test your integrations to ensure everything is
working properly
Notes

PLANNER / BONUS / TECH STACK PLANNER
HOW DO I ADD AND UPGRADE MY
TOOLS?
As noted above, the best way to build your tech stack is to invest in
technology as you need it for the next stage or two of your business then add
and upgrade as you go along.
Here’s how to add or upgrade your technology tools:
✓ 	Add new technology to your stack as needed, keeping in mind the
importance of avoiding shiny objects
✓ 	Before you decide, ask yourself, “Will upgrading to this tool help me
increase sales (or is it just a nice-to-have)?”
✓ 	If you decide to move forward, go back to the start of this planner
and follow the steps to evaluate each tool
Notes:
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
✓ 	Upgrade to new tools as necessary (at some point in your business, you
may have outgrown a tool or realize your current tool is missing
capabilities that can help you grow. For example, once your list reaches
about 20,000, consider switching to an Email Service Provider like
Ontraport or Infusionsoft which give you greater functionality may make
more fiscal sense at this stage
✓ 	Before you decide, ask yourself, “Will upgrading to this tool help me
increase sales (or is it just a nice-to-have)?”
✓ 	If you decide to move forward, go back to the start of this planner
and follow the steps to evaluate each tool
